flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.
Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

attacks.name: Timber Cleave
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Berserker Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

attacks.name: Rapid Strike Squad
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Rapid Strike Pokémon in play.

flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

attacks.name: Brainwave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.
It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

attacks.name: Aqua Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.
The Pokémon is known to bring blizzards. A shake of its massive body is enough to cause whiteout conditions.

rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides ColorlessColorless Energy. The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 less damage to your opponent's Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

All Special Energy attached to Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) provide Colorless Energy and have no other effect.

During this turn, your Fighting Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). During your turn, if this Grant is in your discard pile, you may discard 2 cards, except any Grant, from your hand. If you do, put this Grant into your hand. (This effect doesn't use up your Supporter card for the turn.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Flip 2 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile on top of your deck in any order.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player reveals their hand. Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

fullDisplayName: Wait and See Turbo 158/189 Astral Radiance
rules:
You can use this card only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play. This card can't retreat.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Look at the top card of your deck. You may put that card into your hand. If you don't, discard that card and draw a card.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
All Special Energy attached to Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) provide Colorless Energy and have no other effect.

Switch your Active Basic Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 30 damage from the Pokémon you moved to your Bench.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Choose 1 of your Pokémon, and then flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, heal 40 damage from that Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

When applying Weakness to damage from the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to done to your opponent's Active Pokémon, apply it as ×3.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Your Active Pokémon is now Burned. Heal 40 damage from it.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

You can use this card only if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining. Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 6 cards, and your opponent draws 2 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose a card in your hand, and discard the other cards. If you do, draw 4 cards. (If you have no other cards in your hand, you can't use this card.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
Once during each player's turn, that player may shuffle their hand into their deck and draw 5 cards. If they do, their turn ends.

Search your deck for a Water Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at your face-down Prize cards. You may reveal a Basic Pokémon you find there, put it into your hand, and put this Hisuian Heavy Ball in its place as a face-down Prize card. (If you don't reveal a Basic Pokémon, put this card in the discard pile.) Then, shuffle your face-down Prize cards.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Reveal the top card of your deck. If that card is a Fighting Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If it is not a Fighting Energy card, put it into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

During this turn, your Fighting Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). During your turn, if this Grant is in your discard pile, you may discard 2 cards, except any Grant, from your hand. If you do, put this Grant into your hand. (This effect doesn't use up your Supporter card for the turn.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
Whenever either player puts a Basic Pokémon from their hand onto their Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

Search your deck for a Pokémon that has no Retreat Cost, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Energy card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a basic Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip 2 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile on top of your deck in any order.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player reveals their hand. Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Until the end of your turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon has no Abilities. (This includes Pokémon that come into play during that turn.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

You can use this card only if you discard 2 Metal Energy cards from your hand. Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

flavorText: Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.

attacks.name: Gigaton Break
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Jabber On
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: A versatile performer skilled in the imitation of human speech. It is said that older, more experienced Chatot can even understand the meaning of the words they mimic.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Bewitches humans with its helical tail and piercing gaze. Its hidden claws are quite sharp as well, making this Pokémon an exceedingly tricky opponent if antagonized.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it's been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its strangely shaped antlers have the power to bewitch those who see them. Medicine made by grinding up the black orbs from fallen antlers is an effective treatment for insomnia.

attacks.name: Peat Hunt
attacks.effect: Put up to 2 cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Bulky Bump
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

attacks.name: Continuous Slap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When the cold season arrives in Hisui, this Pokémon will wander fields and mountains alike in search of its favorite berries. Ursaring's hunger during this time makes it a ferocious danger.

attacks.name: Gather Food
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It licks its paws because of the sweet honey that has soaked into them. It is cunning, stealing into the nests of Combee and taking for itself the honey that the Combee have amassed.

attacks.name: Silent Wing
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.
It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.
It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.
An academic theory proposes that Regidrago's arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.

attacks.name: Sonic Strike
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 220 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vengeful Cut
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Pawniard.

flavorText: It's accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

fullDisplayName: Origin Forme Dialga VSTAR 114/189 Astral Radiance

attacks.name: Metal Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Star Chronos
attacks.effect: Take another turn after this one. (Skip Pokémon Checkup.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

fullDisplayName: Origin Forme Dialga V 113/189 Astral Radiance

attacks.name: Metal Coating
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Metal Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Some believe it to be a deity that summons rain clouds. When angered, it lets out a warning cry that rings out like the tolling of a bell.

attacks.name: Metal Press
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Floats using a mysterious energy. The pattern engraved upon its back is held as sacred and can sometimes be found in imagery from ancient cemeteries and other such timeworn places.

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon is from roughly 100 million years ago. Its terrifyingly tough face is harder than steel.

attacks.name: Hard Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although its fossils can be found in layers of primeval rock, nothing but its face has ever been discovered.

attacks.name: Regi Gate
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Heavy Slam
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Registeel's body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.
Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it's being controlled by some mysterious being.

attacks.name: Bounce Back
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is three Magnemite that have linked together. Magneton sends out powerful radio waves to study its surroundings.

attacks.name: Magnetic Catch
attacks.effect: Shuffle up to 3 Metal Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

flavorText: A bizarre Pokémon with but a single eye embedded in an iron sphere. I suspect this creature levitates due to the magnetism it emits from its arms, which resemble horseshoe-shaped magnets.
It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.
Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

attacks.name: Merciless Blade
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 110 more damage.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Basket Crash
attacks.effect: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Mastery
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.

attacks.name: Dark Pulse
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Void
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swirling Disaster
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Dire Claw
attacks.effect: This attack does 80 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.
Its sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught in Sneasel's grasp.

attacks.name: Dirty Press
attacks.effect: If you have at least 3 Darkness Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname "sea fiend." It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

attacks.name: Tormenting Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 5 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

attacks.name: Pinning
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname "sea fiend." It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

attacks.name: Spiny Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.
Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.

attacks.name: Axe Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Timber Cleave
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Berserker Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

attacks.name: Somersault Feathers
attacks.effect: You may discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mountain Hunt
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Close-Quarters Shooting
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Piercing Claw
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Direct Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

attacks.name: Sand Breath
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Stones can get stuck in the ports on their bodies. Dwebble help dislodge such stones, so Hippowdon look after these Pokémon.
Though large and languid, Hippopotas is difficult to detect due to its tendency to burrow into and lurk beneath the soil. When agitated or excited, it expels sand from its nostrils.

attacks.name: Crushing Punch
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jurassic Hammer
attacks.effect: If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This ancient Pokémon used headbutts skillfully. Its brain was really small, so some theories suggest that its stupidity led to its extinction.

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its hard skull is its distinguishing feature. It snapped trees by headbutting them, and then it fed on their ripe berries.

attacks.name: Regi Gate
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Every bit of Regirock's body is made of stone. As parts of its body erode, this Pokémon sticks rocks to itself to repair what's been lost.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo's extreme aversion to water merits special note.

attacks.name: Revenge Buster
attacks.effect: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 140 more damage.

attacks.name: G-Max Chi Strike
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use G-Max Chi Strike.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge Buster
attacks.effect: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 50 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Scorching Horn
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

attacks.name: Defensive Posture
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf all came from the same egg.
It sleeps at the bottom of a lake. Its spirit is said to leave its body to fly on the lake's surface.

attacks.name: Wise Guidance
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It is said that its emergence gave humans the intelligence to improve their quality of life.

attacks.name: Spooky Balloon
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Some say this Pokémon is a collection of souls burdened with regrets, silently drifting through the dusk.

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Said to lure away young children and carry them off to the afterlife. Some whisper that Drifloon are formed of reincarnated human souls, but these rumors are as yet unconfirmed.

attacks.name: Swirling Slice
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: True to its honorable-warrior image, it uses the blades on its elbows only in defense of something or someone.

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Tends to prefer people with a chipper disposition to those who are gloomy, but it has shown no discrimination with regard to age or gender. Needs more research.

attacks.name: Ominous Prose
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. If they have 4 or more cards in their hand, choose all but 3, and your opponent shuffles the chosen cards into their deck.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its muttered curses can cause awful headaches or terrifying visions that torment others.
It conceals itself in darkness, sending chills up travelers' spines with its childlike weeping. As it observes the frightened travelers with glee, the red orbs upon its chest let off an eerie light.
These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they've hardly been seen at all.
They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.
This ovate creature is frequently mistaken for a moving egg when encountered out in the fields or in the mountains. Its guileless smile soothes the soul.

attacks.name: Hollow Flame
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Shimmering Star
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has exactly 4 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Petrifying Flame
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Said to purify lost, forsaken souls with its flames and guide them to the afterlife. I believe its form has been influenced by the energy of the sacred mountain towering at Hisui's center.

attacks.name: Electromagnetic Sonar
attacks.effect: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Targeted Bolt
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon is a cluster of electrical energy. It's said that removing the rings on Regieleki's body will unleash the Pokémon's latent power.

attacks.name: Fang Snipe
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a Trainer card you find there.

attacks.name: Radiating Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

fullDisplayName: Galarian Mr. Rime V 049/189 Astral Radiance

attacks.name: Surprising Hand
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Customized Cane
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mountain Gale
attacks.effect: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

flavorText: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.
Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.

attacks.name: Moonlight Shuriken
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: It appears and vanishes with a ninja's grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

attacks.name: Line Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

attacks.name: Grudge Dive
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

attacks.name: Gather the Crew
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

attacks.name: Razor Shell
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its exquisite double-scalchop technique is likely the result of daily training, and it can send even masters of the blade fleeing in defeat.
This Pokémon from the Unova region uses the shell on its belly as a weapon to cut down its foes. Thus, I've conferred upon this shell the name "scalchop.".

fullDisplayName: Origin Forme Palkia VSTAR 040/189 Astral Radiance

attacks.name: Subspace Swell
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

fullDisplayName: Origin Forme Palkia V 039/189 Astral Radiance

attacks.name: Rule the Region
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hydro Break
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frost Wall
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.

flavorText: Glaceon is able to lower its body temperature very quickly. It freezes the atmosphere, creating diamond dust that glitters like gems while it flutters and dances around.

attacks.name: Regi Gate
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Blizzard Bind
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: With cold air that can reach temperatures as low as −328 degrees Fahrenheit, Regice instantly freezes any creature that approaches it.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.
Makes its home in swamps with murky water. The poor visibility hides this Pokémon from predators, and the slime on its body makes grasping it difficult.

attacks.name: Borne Ashore
attacks.effect: Put a Basic Pokémon from either player's discard pile onto that player's Bench.

flavorText: This calm and gentle Pokémon swims gracefully through the sea. After building speed, it can leap out of the water. It is often misidentified as a bird Pokémon due to this behavior.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Iceberg Press
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Swinub excels at sniffing out mushrooms buried beneath grass or snow. Since ancient times, the people of Hisui have often relied upon this skill.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Spiral
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Entangled Dive
attacks.effect: Discard each player's Active Pokémon and all attached cards. (You choose a new Active Pokémon first.)

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in gently flowing rivers. It paddles through the water with its long limbs, putting its graceful swimming skills on display.

attacks.name: Spacing Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Suffers perpetual headaches. If the agony grows too great, Psyduck's latent power erupts, contrary to Psyduck's intent. Ergo, I am exploring ways to ease the pain.

attacks.name: Raging Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

attacks.name: Max Heat Burst
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heat Burn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Magma Fall
attacks.effect: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: This creature's fur is most mysterious—it is wholly impervious to the burning touch of flame. Should Quilava turn its back to you, take heed! Such a posture indicates a forthcoming attack.

attacks.name: Charge Energy
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Hails from the Johto region. Though usually curled into a ball due to its timid disposition, it harbors tremendous firepower.

attacks.name: Ring of Fire
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.
These Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.
Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.

attacks.name: Parallel Spin
attacks.effect: You may put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. If you do, this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dance Gracefully
attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Twister Lutz
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The leaves on its head are highly valued for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves in the sun, boil them, and then drink the bitter decoction for remarkably effective relief from fatigue.

attacks.name: Encouraging Gift
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When the turning of seasons brings the cruel winter to its end and the joyous people give thanks to the heavens, Shaymin appears and covers the withered land with flowers.

attacks.name: Energy Garden
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Leafy Cyclone
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Cells similar to those of plants have been found in its fur. Its hard tail can fell a large tree with one stroke, and the tail's sharpness exceeds even that of a sword crafted by a master.

attacks.name: Honey Rush
attacks.effect: Reveal any number of Sweet Honey cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you revealed in this way.

flavorText: It skillfully commands its grubs in battles with its enemies. The grubs are willing to risk their lives to defend Vespiquen.

attacks.name: Honey Courier
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The members of the trio spend all their time together. Each one has a slightly different taste in nectar.
It uses its cutlass-like arms to produce sound, the melody of which varies from individual to individual. It is a worthwhile endeavor to seek out one's favorite tunes.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

attacks.name: Overhead Throw
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon has an unparalleled horn. Heracross itself demonstrates tremendous power—it's capable of throwing several people trained in the traditional arts of war at once.

attacks.name: Wide Wing
attacks.effect: If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.
Its frail wings are so thin that one can see clear through them. However, during flight these wings exhibit the power to churn air with force enough to launch a house skyward.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey's attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon's nimble motions.
The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey's attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon's nimble motions.

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

flavorText: The tissue on the surface of its body is curiously similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, this Pokémon lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Cheerful Charge
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Swarming Sting
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each of your Beedrill V in play. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
